novel_features.name: What changed since last sync
novel_features.description: See only the tweets curated or newly hot since your last sync, instead of re-reading the whole stream.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a persisted local sync cursor; the API has no "delta since timestamp" call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer "what did I miss" without re-fetching or re-scanning the full corpus.

novel_features.name: Topic momentum over time
novel_features.description: Show which topics are rising, falling, or newly appearing across the heatmap snapshots stored on each sync.
novel_features.rationale: Requires historical heatmap snapshots accumulated locally; the live heatmap is a single snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when the question is about change over time, which no single API call can answer.

novel_features.name: Emerging-narrative detector
novel_features.description: Surface keywords that newly emerged or accelerated versus prior snapshots, grounded in supporting stored tweets.
novel_features.rationale: Requires diffing keyword sets across stored snapshots; the live narrative-alert is only a snapshot.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this over a one-shot narrative pull when you want what is *newly* emerging, not just what is currently large.

novel_features.name: Author engagement leaderboard
novel_features.description: Rank authors by accumulated stored engagement over a window, each with their best curated tweet.
novel_features.rationale: Requires per-author engagement summed across stored syncs plus each author's best stored tweet.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this for a leaderboard that compounds as you sync, not the API's one-shot day winner.

novel_features.name: Time travel by date
novel_features.description: Show the curated tweets for a specific date (YYYY-MM-DD, today, yesterday, or latest), live or fully offline from the local store.
novel_features.rationale: Ports the homepage Time Travel panel and serves it from the local mirror, so any past day is browsable without re-fetching.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this to pull the snapshot of what tech Twitter was saying on a given day, with no live call once synced.

novel_features.name: Offline composed digest
novel_features.description: Assemble a read-list from the local store for a window: top tweets, recent articles, and top authors.
novel_features.rationale: Requires cross-entity local composition; the API has no single endpoint that returns this blend.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to hand an agent a cited, single-shot "what's happening in tech" rollup with zero extra calls.

novel_features.name: Offline cited evidence bundle
novel_features.description: Build an evidence bundle mirroring the agent-context kinds from local SQLite, with canonical-URL citations and no network.
novel_features.rationale: Mirrors the 6 agent-context kinds but assembles from the local store, so it works fully offline.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use this when an agent needs cited evidence but you want it grounded in the local mirror with no upstream call.

narrative.headline: Every Tech Twitter read endpoint, plus an offline-searchable local mirror and cited evidence bundles no other tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Tech Twitter curates, dedupes, summarizes, and quality-scores the high-signal slice of tech X. This CLI turns that into a local SQLite mirror you can full-text search offline, and adds commands that only a compounding store can answer: what changed since your last sync, topic momentum over time, emerging narratives, time travel to any past day, and offline cited evidence bundles for agents.

quickstart.comment: Confirm the CLI and its reachability config before anything else.
Pull the curated stream into the local SQLite mirror.
Full-text search the curated corpus offline.
Pull the live ranked trending stream as JSON.
Compose an offline read-list for the last 24 hours.

troubleshoots.fix: The CLI must send a browser-shaped User-Agent; bare scraper UAs are blocked on all non-machine paths. The default client already does this — only override --user-agent with a browser string.

troubleshoots.symptom: A command redirects or returns the homepage
troubleshoots.fix: That endpoint is auth-gated and out of scope for the public CLI. Use the documented public commands (search, trending, latest, author, topic, articles, products, agent context).
Run techtwitter-pp-cli sync first to populate the local mirror; since/momentum/digest read from stored data.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or user needs cited, structured evidence from Tech Twitter's curated tech-discourse corpus, when you want offline full-text search over a deduped quality-scored stream instead of the raw X firehose, or when the question is about change over time (what's new, what's gaining momentum, what narrative is emerging) that a single stateless API call cannot answer.
anti_triggers: Posting, liking, bookmarking, or otherwise writing to X/Twitter — this CLI is read-only over Tech Twitter's curated corpus, not an X client.
Fetching arbitrary tweets straight from X — only tweets Tech Twitter has curated are available.
Account, billing, sponsor, or submission flows on techtwitter.com — those are auth-gated and out of scope.

recipes.title: Cited what-changed evidence for an agent
recipes.explanation: Pulls a cited evidence bundle and narrows the deeply nested response to just title, citation URL, and quality score so an agent isn't flooded with media and metric fields.

recipes.title: Daily tech standup, offline
recipes.explanation: Composes a read-list of top tweets, recent articles, and top authors for the last day from the local mirror, agent-formatted with citations.

recipes.title: What is newly emerging
recipes.explanation: Diffs stored heatmap snapshots to surface keywords that just emerged or accelerated, each grounded in supporting stored tweets.

recipes.title: Search the curated corpus offline
recipes.explanation: Full-text searches the synced local store with no network call, returning quality-scored curated tweets as JSON.

recipes.title: Track a topic's momentum
recipes.explanation: Shows which topics are rising, falling, or newly appearing across the snapshots stored over the last week of syncs.

trigger_phrases: what narrative is emerging in AI
